name: Sarah Roland, Attorney at Law

notableCases.name: Michael Lorence v. The State of Texas (No. 02-15-00398-CR, Tex. App.—Fort Worth; Nancy Howard murder-for-hire prosecution)
notableCases.outcome: 2015 aggravated assault conviction reversed on appeal in 2017; on retrial January 23, 2019 the client was found not guilty of Aggravated Assault with a Deadly Weapon.

websiteQuality.rationale: Modern, actively maintained WordPress 6.9.4/Elementor site with HTTPS, responsive viewport meta, fresh 2026 copyright, current blog content, and per-topic practice-area pages. Trust signals are excellent (Super Lawyer 2014-2026 badge, Avvo Superb, TCDLA leadership, client testimonial, Organization JSON-LD schema) and phone CTAs with a free-consultation offer appear on every page with an embedded Google Map for local positioning. The deliberate absence of a contact form or online booking (the firm states calls get faster answers) and mostly empty image alt attributes are the main weaknesses.

factors.mobileUsability: Responsive: viewport meta tag present and Elementor configured with additional custom breakpoints; navigation collapses to mobile menu.
factors.pageSpeed: Not directly measured (no lab tooling). Inferred moderate: ~104 KB HTML homepage built on WordPress + Elementor with Google Fonts and multiple page-builder scripts/stylesheets, typical of Elementor sites.
factors.accessibility: Weak-to-average: several images have empty alt attributes (only a few descriptive alts such as the Super Lawyers badge); no accessibility statement observed; not formally audited.
factors.visualCredibility: Professional modern design with attorney photography, recognition badges, and a featured client testimonial; content is current (2026 copyright, recent blog posts).
factors.navigation: Clear header nav with dropdowns for 14 practice areas, About/Meet the Team, Notable Cases, Blawg, and Contact; consistent footer with address, phone, email.
factors.contentClarity: Plain-language, client-focused explanations of charges and process; dedicated page per offense type plus educational blog content.
factors.callsToAction: Phone number in meta description, header, and repeated 'Call for a free consultation' CTAs across pages; contact page leads with the phone number.
factors.contactFormQuality: No contact form by design — the contact page states forms are too slow for criminal matters and directs visitors to call; email address published in footer.
factors.localPositioning: Strong: 'Denton County Criminal Defense Attorney' in titles, explicit Denton/Collin/Cooke county focus, embedded Google Map of the Elm Street office.
factors.trustSignals: Super Lawyer 2014-2026 (2026 badge), Avvo 'Superb' rating, America's Top 100 Criminal Defense Attorneys, TCDLA Voice editor/executive committee, Innocence Project of Texas board service, awards list, client testimonial, Organization/WebSite JSON-LD schema.
factors.appointmentBooking: None — no online scheduler or booking widget; intake is phone-first with a free consultation.

sources.supports: Firm branding, phone (940) 323-9305, street address, published email (mailto), practice focus, trust signals, site technology and quality signals

notes: Site brands the firm simply as 'Sarah Roland' while its own embedded Google Maps listing and directories use 'Sarah Roland, Attorney at Law'; also styled 'Law Office of Sarah Roland' on the About page. Seed fact 'solo' is outdated — the firm's About page lists associate George Roland III (Sarah's brother). Super Lawyers profile lists a different phone (940-222-2006); the current site uses (940) 323-9305. Sarah's late father George Roland was also a Denton criminal defense lawyer, referenced on the About page. KERA News (2023) quoted Roland regarding a client awaiting competency restoration — newsworthy but unnamed, so not listed as a notable case.
